gpt55_review_summary: Initial review found that the documented databricks_runs get --summary wrapper would be rejected by release_bundle, so I normalized wrapper and root status shapes and updated tests/docs. Re-review then found a misplaced helper had made a CLI test unreachable; I moved the helper back to fixtures, reran exact/focused/full tests plus build, and final review returned clean.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py
GPT-5.5 review clean after resolving wrapper-shape and test-regression findings
what_changed: Added repeatable Databricks run-status sidecars to release bundles.
Validated compact document_kv.databricks_run_status.v1 records, including actual databricks_runs get --summary wrapper outputs, before bundling.
Kept Databricks status sidecars out of strict release-evidence and preflight input matching while preserving semantic record_type provenance in the bundle manifest.
why: Release bundles should carry auditable proof that Databricks-managed benchmark and probe jobs reached successful terminal states without requiring operators to persist full Jobs API payloads.
